page: Amazon Web Services z1d.3xlarge is a memory-optimized and high-frequency compute instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8151 processor with 12 dedicated vCPUs (6 physical cores) operating at a sustained clock speed of 4.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 96.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance is equipped with a 450 GB NVMe SSD. Network performance is defined by a baseline bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations place the z1d.3xlarge in the average performance tier across multiple workloads. The processor achieves a stress-ng single-core score of 1658.59 and a multi-core score of 13093.09, supported by a 24 MB L3 cache and AVX-512 instruction sets. Memory read-only bandwidth reaches 729.7 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 66.01 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. In database operations, the instance handles 12.59 million Redis SET operations per second and scores 4668.02 on PassMark database tests. For language model tasks, it processes 802.99 tokens per second for a small 135M model, though prompt processing performance drops to a weak tier of 19.32 tokens per second for a medium 7B model.

The z1d.3xlarge offers a balanced cost-to-resource profile for workloads requiring high single-core CPU frequency alongside high memory density. The inclusion of local NVMe SSD storage reduces the need for external storage attachments for temporary data. However, tradeoffs include a relatively low baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps and the absence of a dedicated GPU, which limits its utility for large-scale deep learning. This instance is best suited for relational databases, memory-intensive applications, electronic design automation, and high-frequency financial simulations.
description: Amazon Web Services z1d.3xlarge is an x86_64 memory-optimized instance featuring a high-frequency Intel Xeon 8151 processor. It delivers 12 dedicated vCPUs (6 physical cores) running at 4.0 GHz, paired with 96.0 GB of DDR4 RAM and a 450 GB local NVMe SSD. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance achieves average-tier benchmark results, including a stress-ng multi-core score of 13093.09 and 12.59 million Redis SET operations per second. While it offers high single-core clock speeds and a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, tradeoffs include a baseline network speed of 5 Gbps and weak prompt processing performance on medium 7B LLM models. The z1d.3xlarge is designed for memory-intensive workloads, relational databases, and high-frequency computing tasks that benefit from fast local storage and high clock speeds without requiring GPU acceleration.
og_description: Amazon Web Services z1d.3xlarge is an x86_64 memory-optimized instance featuring 12 vCPUs at 4.0 GHz, 96 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 450 GB local NVMe SSD, designed for high-frequency database workloads.

tagline: A high-frequency, memory-optimized compute instance featuring dedicated cores, fast local NVMe storage, and balanced database performance.
bullet_points: 12 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8151 processor at 4.0 GHz
96.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing an 8.0 GB per core ratio
450 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for low-latency data access
Average-tier database performance with 12.59 million Redis SET operations per second
Suited for relational databases, EDA, and memory-intensive applications
